properties.name: Pali Dyeing-Printing Water Pollution
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Dyeing-printing industries at Pali have made the Luni river one of Rajasthan's worst-polluted water bodies.
Chromium, dyes and detergent chemicals from Pali's Mandia and Punayata industrial estates cross the Central Pollution Control Board's effluent thresholds regularly.
Downstream Luni water is unusable for agriculture — a case study cited in the Class 12 syllabus.

properties.name: Sanganer Dyeing-Print Groundwater Contamination
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Sanganeri hand-block printing and washing use the Amanishah drain — recharge from it has contaminated Sanganer's groundwater.
Salinity, iron and dye residues make Sanganer aquifer water unfit for drinking within the industrial zone.
Traditional two-colour (red-black) natural-dye Sanganeri print survives with GI status — the pollution is from newer chemical-dye workshops.

properties.name: Kota Super Thermal Plant Air Pollution
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: The Kota Super Thermal Plant (KSTPS) at the city's edge drives Rajasthan's single largest urban air-pollution footprint.
Fly ash spreads 1-2 km into surrounding agricultural land; the Kota-Ramganjmandi limestone belt adds cement-dust drift.
Suratgarh and Chhabra thermal plants are the state's two other major SPM and SO₂ sources.

properties.name: Chambal Industrial Water Pollution (Kota)
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Rajasthan's only perennial river — the Chambal — has been placed under a formal Chambal Purification Programme against industrial-effluent pollution.
Kota-based fertiliser and chemical plants (Chambal Fertilisers, IPCL) are the main downstream contributors.
The pollution here directly threatens the National Chambal Sanctuary (gharial + Gangetic dolphin habitat).

properties.name: Chittor–Nimbahera Cement Belt Air Pollution
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: The Nimbahera–Chittorgarh–Chanderiya cement belt hosts J.K. White, Wonder, Ultra-Tech and Birla Cement clinker plants.
Cement dust deposition within 1-2 km of the plants reduces soil fertility across Rajasthan's largest cement corridor.
Vindhyan-limestone quarries here feed the belt — the cluster is Rajasthan's largest single-industry air-pollution source outside Kota.

properties.name: Bhilwara Textile Effluent
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Bhilwara ("Manchester of Rajasthan") — India's largest suiting-fabric hub — discharges textile-mill effluents into the Kothari (Banas tributary) sub-basin.
Chemical dyes and processing wash-water have been repeatedly flagged by the Rajasthan State Pollution Control Board as non-compliant with CETP norms.
The Angucha-Dariba lead-zinc belt to the north-east adds heavy-metal load in the same catchment.

properties.name: Jhamarkotra Rock-Phosphate Mine — Dust & Runoff
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: India's largest rock-phosphate mine (RSMML, Jhamarkotra) — critical to India's phosphatic-fertiliser sovereignty.
Open-cast blasting and heavy machinery raise SPM by up to 5x background within a 3 km radius.
Acid-mine drainage risks contaminating the Ahar river headwaters — a permanent Udaipur-basin concern.

properties.name: Khetri Copper Belt Pollution
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Khetri copper concentrator + HCL smelter emit SO₂ and heavy-metal dust — historically the state's dirtiest metal-mining hub.
Tailings-pond seepage has raised copper and cadmium in Kolihan groundwater to unsafe levels for decades.
The belt was Rajasthan's first mining site to receive Central Pollution Control Board action orders (1980s).

properties.name: Sambhar Salt Lake — Ecological Stress
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: India's largest inland saline lake — encroachment by illegal salt-pan operators has shrunk the water body over the last 30 years.
2019 mass die-off of migratory birds (Sambhar avian botulism) killed 25,000+ ducks, geese and waders in three weeks.
Rajasthan Government notified Sambhar as a wetland-conservation site under the Ramsar framework in 2022.

properties.name: Aravalli Illegal Mining — Alwar-Rajgarh Belt
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: The Supreme Court has repeatedly ordered a halt to illegal quarrying in the Rajgarh-Neemli belt (Alwar) — the Aravalli's northern rampart.
Deforestation and pit-mining here open the "Delhi-Alwar gap" that funnels desert dust storms into the NCR.
A key case-study in the Central Empowered Committee's Aravalli reports since 2018.

properties.name: Fluorosis Belt — Jalore-Barmer-Nagaur
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Groundwater fluoride tops 10 PPM in 1,452 Rajasthani villages — Jalore, Banswara, Barmer, Jaisalmer, Nagaur and Sirohi are worst affected.
Fluorosis symptoms: dental mottling, skeletal fluorosis (hunchback), joint stiffness, bone-decay.
Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 flags this as the state's most systemic drinking-water crisis.

properties.name: Mangarh Hill Sacred Forest Regeneration
properties.source: Rajasthan Adhyan Class 12 Ch 6 (Environmental Challenges) + RSPCB + CGWB

facts: Following the National Mangarh Dham memorial dedication (2022), the Rajasthan Government notified an eco-restoration project for the hill.
Restored dry-deciduous forest cover — Bhil-community-led plantation of local dhok, salar and khejri.
A rare case where the state's freedom-struggle memorialisation directly triggered environmental recovery.
